manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: mango, topaz, igloo, onion, cucumber, uncle, asparagus, mace, lamp.
Your opponent's team words are: plum, hotel, armoire, herbs, house, ankle, mother, gem.
Distractor words are: axe, apricot, desk, face, grape, cumin, son.
The kill words are: sage.

Let me reconsider - is there any risk with "nutmeg"?
- Could lead to "herbs" - unlikely, nutmeg and mace are spices, not herbs
- Could lead to "sage" - unlikely, sage is an herb, not related to nutmeg


- Could lead to "cumin" - unlikely, these are different spices
- Could lead to "apricot", "grape" - no connection

"Nutmeg" seems like the safest option. It's specific to the spice meaning and avoids triggering any of the dangerous words. The weapon interpretation with "medieval" carries more risk since they might not make the connection clearly.

I'll go with "nutmeg".

content.thinking:
The word is 'nutmeg'. Let me think about what relates to nutmeg:

- Nutmeg is a spice
- Looking at the list: apricot, grape, mace, cumin, face, armoire, herbs, axe, desk, son, sage

Words that could relate to nutmeg:
- mace: This is actually the outer covering of the nutmeg seed! Mace and nutmeg come from the same plant. This is very closely associated.
- cumin: This is also a spice, so related
- herbs: Spices and herbs are related categories
- sage: This is an herb/spice

But "mace" is the most closely associated because mace is literally the aril (the reddish covering) of the nutmeg seed. They come from the same fruit of the nutmeg tree. This is the strongest association.

I can only choose 1 word, so I'll go with "mace".
